reasoning: The forecast compares the spread between Moody's Aaa Corporate Bond Yield and the 10-year Treasury yield on three future resolution dates against its value on 2025-11-09. The most recent available data as of the cutoff (2025-11-07) was 1.16, which represents a short-term local peak up from ~1.05 in late October. Consequently, local mean reversion suggests the spread is more likely to drop in the very near term. The FRED statistical tool's linear trend analysis predicts a reversion to 1.129 by mid-November, supporting a low probability of an increase by Nov 16. Our base rate anchor yields P(value > 1.16) = 0.34 for the nearest horizon. Over the longer term, corporate bond spreads tend to approximate a random walk with wide variance, so the short-term mean-reversion pull becomes less dominant. Following the instruction that uncertainty must increase (moving closer to 0.5) over longer horizons, the probabilities have been stair-stepped towards 0.5 to reflect the growing variance and decreasing reliance on the immediate short-term peak of 1.16.
